factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in several segments.

evidence.text: DTS total revenue increased 12% reflecting higher operating revenue and increased subcontracted transportation. SCS total revenue increased 10% due to higher operating revenue and increased subcontracted transportation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand acceleration in multiple segments indicates growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: SCS total revenue increased 10% with growth in CPG/Retail and Technology/Healthcare. DTS total revenue increased 12% with higher operating revenue and new business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments suggests improved profitability.

evidence.text: DTS operating revenue increased 4% due to increased volumes and new business. SCS operating revenue increased 6% largely due to new business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth acceleration from new business and volume increases.

evidence.text: Total vehicles serviced increased 1% to 250,300 in the first quarter. Average fleet increased 2% to 8,500 in DTS and 9% to 8,400 in SCS.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing unit volumes reflect expanding operations.

factors.summary: End markets are growing, particularly in DTS and SCS.

evidence.text: DTS total revenue increased 12% with strength in dedicated transportation. SCS total revenue increased 10% with growth in CPG/Retail and Technology/Healthcare.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong end-market growth in key segments supports expansion.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly fuel costs.

evidence.text: Fuel costs increased 35% in SCS and 2% in DTS. These costs are being passed through to customers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising fuel costs pose a financial challenge.

factors.summary: Margins are mixed with some improvement and some compression.

evidence.text: DTS EBT increased 16% with revenue growth and operating performance. SCS EBT decreased 7% due to lower volumes in automotive and ongoing issues in one customer account.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed margin performance across segments indicates uncertainty.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are increasing for new vehicles.

evidence.text: NYE units increased 41% compared to March 31, 2017, reflecting lease fleet growth.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Inventory increases may signal supply chain issues or market softness.

factors.summary: Management is confident with growth and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We expect full-year 2018 capital expenditures to be approximately $3 billion. We expect revenue comparisons to remain favorable through the end of the year.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses confidence in growth and capital expenditures with clear expectations.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include higher used vehicle inventories and potential fuel cost volatility.

evidence.text: We expect NLE levels to increase slightly through the end of the year as a result of higher expected used vehicle inventories driven by the lease vehicle replacement cycle. Fuel costs increased significantly in both DTS and SCS.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher used vehicle inventories and fuel cost volatility pose moderate risks.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel costs and foreign exchange rates.

evidence.text: Fuel costs increased 35% in SCS and 2% in DTS. Foreign exchange impacted SCS revenue by 1%.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Fuel costs and foreign exchange are highlighted as key sensitivities without clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable with strong sales activity.

evidence.text: We expect revenue comparisons to remain favorable through the end of the year based on strong sales activity.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong sales activity supports favorable economic conditions.

factors.summary: CapEx is increasing, signaling confidence in growth.

evidence.text: We expect full-year 2018 capital expenditures to be approximately $3 billion. Capital expenditures increased 63% to $711 million in the three months ended March 31, 2018.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant increase in capital expenditures signals strong confidence in growth.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is negative, indicating cash outflow.

evidence.text: Free cash flow was $(238,157) million in the three months ended March 31, 2018, compared to $82,784 million in 2017.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative free cash flow indicates cash outflow, which is a clear headwind.
